```bash
anything
```

THOUGHT: The miscompilation arises because InstCombine is transforming a select based on an fcmp ult into a llvm.minnum call even when the instruction has the nsz (no signed zeros) fast-math flag. The original select preserves the sign of zero from %x (+0), while llvm.minnum can produce -0 under nsz semantics, causing a mismatch. To fix this, we should modify InstCombine to avoid converting such select patterns into minnum/maxnum when nsz is set, or ensure the transformation respects zero-sign behavior. I will locate the relevant InstCombine code and adjust the condition to prohibit this transformation when nsz is present.

```bash
grep -Rn --exclude-dir=build --exclude-dir=.git -E "minnum|maxnum|fmin|fmax|select.*fcmp" llvm | sed -n '1,200p'
```

THOUGHT: The miscompile happens because InstCombine transforms a select+fcmp into minnum/maxnum when the select has the NSZ fast-math flag. This changes the sign of zero (+0 to -0), which the original select would preserve, causing Alive2 to report a mismatch. To fix, I will tighten the condition in InstCombineSelect.cpp to not allow the minnum/maxnum canonicalization solely based on NSZ. Instead, require explicit proof that users ignore zero sign (via canIgnoreSignBitOfZero), preventing incorrect -0 propagation.

```bash
sed -i '3932,3936s/SIFPOp->hasNoNaNs() &&\n        (SIFPOp->hasNoSignedZeros() ||\n         (SIFPOp->hasOneUse() &&\n          canIgnoreSignBitOfZero(*SIFPOp->use_begin())))/SIFPOp->hasNoNaNs() &&\n        (SIFPOp->hasOneUse() && canIgnoreSignBitOfZero(*SIFPOp->use_begin()))/' llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineSelect.cpp && nl -ba llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineSelect.cpp | sed -n '3928,3958p'
```

THOUGHT: The incorrect transformation stems from InstCombine allowing select+fcmp to be canonicalized into minnum/maxnum when NSZ is set, which permits changing +0 to -0. We should only allow this transform when we can prove users ignore the sign of zero, not merely because NSZ is present. I'll modify the condition in InstCombineSelect.cpp to require canIgnoreSignBitOfZero (with one use), removing the NSZ shortcut.

```bash
sed -i '3932,3935s/.*/        if (SIFPOp->hasNoNaNs() && SIFPOp->hasOneUse() && canIgnoreSignBitOfZero(*SIFPOp->use_begin())) {/' llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineSelect.cpp && submit-patch
```


  %cmp1 = fcmp ogt double %x, 9.000000e-01
  %sel1 = select i1 %cmp1, double 9.000000e-01, double %x
  %cmp2 = fcmp olt double %sel1, 5.000000e-01
  %sel2 = select i1 %cmp2, double 5.000000e-01, double %sel1
  ret double %sel2
}

THOUGHT: The failing tests expect the newly created minnum/maxnum intrinsics to carry the same fast-math flags (including nsz) as the original select. Instead of manually setting individual flags, I will copy the fast-math flags from the select instruction to the created intrinsic using copyFastMathFlags. This should restore expected behavior and satisfy the tests.
